methods.description: This method involves collecting and preparing pollen samples from various sources for analysis. The samples are preserved and processed to ensure accurate analysis of pollen grain structure.

steps.description: Personal collections as well as loans and gifts from various institutions and individuals provided the pollen samples.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen samples from various sources

steps.description: Pollen preserved in formalin-acetic acid-70 percent ethyl alcohol was available for all species and other samples were obtained from dried herbarium collections.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve pollen samples in formalin-acetic acid-70% ethyl alcohol

methods.description: This method describes the processing of pollen samples to analyze the fine structure of pollen grains, using various staining and mounting techniques.

steps.description: All samples of dried pollen were successfully expanded in 50 percent alcohol solution at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: expand dried pollen in 50% alcohol solution

steps.description: Two portions were cleared in 2.5 percent NaOH at room temperature for 2-3 days and washed in distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: clear pollen in 2.5% NaOH and wash in distilled water

steps.description: One portion then was stained in 1 percent aqueous solution of safranin 0, mounted in distilled water, and sealed with a beeswax-paraffin mixture or commercial fingernail polish.
steps.searchable_summary: stain pollen with safranin 0 and mount in distilled water

steps.description: The other portion was stained in a 50 percent ethyl alcohol solution containing 1 percent safranin 0, destained in absolute alcohol, transferred to xylene, and mounted in canada balsam for permanent slides.
steps.searchable_summary: stain and mount pollen for permanent slides

steps.description: The third portion was dehydrated in an ethyl alcohol series and mounted in a mixture of safranin 0 and glycerine jelly.
steps.searchable_summary: dehydrate and mount pollen in safranin 0 and glycerine jelly

steps.description: The fourth portion was washed in distilled water, stained in a 1 percent aqueous solution of ruthenium red, and mounted in distilled water.
steps.searchable_summary: stain pollen with ruthenium red and mount in distilled water

methods.description: This method involves preparing pollen grains for electron microscopy to study their fine structure.

steps.description: Fresh pollen grains were fixed in a 5 percent phosphate buffered gluteraldehyde solution and post-fixed in a 5 percent phosphate buffered osmium tetroxide solution.
steps.searchable_summary: fix pollen grains in gluteraldehyde and osmium tetroxide

steps.description: Following alcoholic and propylene oxide dehydration, they were imbedded in a mixture of Araldite and Epon.
steps.searchable_summary: dehydrate and embed pollen in Araldite and Epon

steps.description: Thin sections were cut using glass knives in a Porter-Blum microtome MT-1.
steps.searchable_summary: cut thin sections of pollen using microtome

steps.description: Observations and electronmicrographs were made with a Philips E. M. 75 and a Siemen's Elmiskop 1 A.
